December 7, 1983 
Stotements of Economic Interest 
322-6444 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice, Our 
Advice No. A-83-253 
Mr. Pat Hashem, a candidate for the City Council of the City 
of Calexico, has requested that you seek our advice as to a 
possible conflict of interest situation which might arise if the 
candidate is successful in being elected to the Council in April 
1984. 
The facts are as follows: 
FACTS 
1. 
On August 16, 1982, the candidate entered into a 
land lease agreement with the City of Calexico for a period 
of ten (10) years. 
The lease will terminate on August 16, 
1992. 
He is currently paying a monthly rental of $650. 
2. 
The candidate has announced his intent to run for a 
council seat in the April 1984 Municipal Elections. 
If 
elected in the April 1984 Municipal Elections, the 
candidate's term would be from April 1984 to April 1988. 
3. 
If the candidate is successful in the April 1984 
Municipal Elections, a conflict of interest would be 
created, pursuant to {Calexico's] Conflict of Interest Code, 
requiring disclosure and disqualification of any action 
pertaining to the candidate's lease. 
QUESTION 
You have requested the Commission's advice on the following 
question: 
Apart from disclosure and disqualification from any 
action, would any other liability be created under the 
applicable Government Code Conflict of Interest sections? 


Eduardo A. Rivera 
December 7, 1983 
Page 2 
In addition, in your first and then second letters you have 
asked our advice regarding the possible implications under 
Government Code Section 1090, et~. Because we do not 
administer or enforce that provislon of the law,ll we are 
unable to advise you as to those questions. 
You may wish to 
consult with the Attorney General on that point. 
ANALYSIS 
The political Reform Act provides that: 
No public official at any level of state or local 
government shall make, participate in making or in any 
way attempt to use his official position to influence a 
governmental decision in which he knows or has reason 
to know he has a financial interest~ 
Section 87100. 
A financial interest in a decision exists whenever there 
will be a reasonably foreseeable material financial effect, 
distinguishable from the effect on the public generally, on: 
(b) 
Any real property in which the public 
official has a direct or indirect interest worth more 
than one thousand dollars ($1,000). 
An "interest in real property" is defined in Section 82033 
to include "any leasehold ••• interest ••• in real property 
located in the jurisdiction • ••• " 
Clearly, in this situation the 
leasehold has a value in excess of $1,000. 
However, because the 
leasehold has less than 10 years remaining, it will not be 
reportable under the provisions of Section 82033 should the 
candidate be elected and take office in 1984. 
However, the 
leasehold interest could form the basis for possible 
disqualification.~1 
II The Fair Political Practices Commission administers, 
implements, enforces and interprets the Political Reform Act of 
1974 (Government Code Sections 81000-91014). 
Government Code 
Sections 83111-83116. 
All statutory references are to the 
Government Code unless otherwise specified. 
2/ 3ee the Commission's Overstreet Opinion, 6 FPPC 
Opinions 12, No. 80-010, March 2, 1981, copy enclosed. 


Eduardo A. Rivera 
December 7, 1983 
Page 3 
Disqualification of Mr. Pat Hashem would be required as to 
any decision which would have a material financial effect1/ on 
his leasehold interest where that effect is distinguishable from 
the decision's effect upon the public generally.i/ If 
Mr. Hashem becomes a member of the City Council of Calexico and 
a specific decision presents itself raising this issue, do not 
hesitate to again seek our advice. 
If you have any questions regarding this letter, I may be 
reached at 916/322-5901. 
REL: km 
Enclosures 
Sincerely, 
Robert E. Leidigh 
Counsel, Legal Division

A

interest are as follows: 
1. 
On August 16, 1982, the candidate entered 
into a land lease agreement with the City of 
Calexico for a period of ten (10) years. 
The 
lease will terminate on August 16, 1992. 
He 
is currently paying a monthly rental of $650. 
2. 
The candidate has announced his intent to 
run for a council seat in the April 1983 
Municipal Elections. 
If elected in the April 
1983 Municipal Elections, the candidate's 
term would be from April 1984 to April 1988. 
3. 
If the candidate is successful in the April 
1983 Municipal Elections, a conflict of interest 
would be created, pursuant to our Conflict of 
Interest Code, requiring disclosure and dis-
qualification of any action pertaininq to the 
candidate's lease. 
The opinion requested from the Commission is as follows: 
1. 
Apart from disclosure and disqualification 
from any action, would any other liability be 
created under the applicable Government Code 
Conflict of Interest sections?

Would Government Code Section 1090 require that 
the candidate, if successful, relinquish his lease 
with the City of Calexico? 
Would you be kind enough to provide me with comments on the 
above two (2) questions. 
If you should have any further questions, please feel free to 
contact this office.

regarding a conflict of interest in regards to a 
candidate for a City of Calexico Council seat, a copy

A further opinion is 
requested as to the Government Code Section 1090 issue:

Government Code Section 1090 would indicate 
that the candidate, if successful, should 
relinquish his lease, would it be possible 
for the candidate to have his lease operated 
and/or administrated under some allowable 
trust arrangement. 
We have enclosed a copy 
of his lease agreement to aid you in this 
determination. 
If you should have any further questions, please feel 
free to contact this office.

sections.question:
You have requested the Commission's advice on the following 
question: 
Apart from disclosure and disqualification from any 
action, would any other liability be created under the 
applicable Government Code Conflict of Interest sections? 


Eduardo A. Rivera 
December 7, 1983  
In addition, in your first and then second letters you have 
asked our advice regarding the possible implications under 
Government Code Section 1090, et~. Because we do not 
administer or enforce that provislon of the law,ll we are 
unable to advise you as to those questions. 
You may wish to 
consult with the Attorney General on that point.
sections.conclusion: Disqualification of Mr. Pat Hashem would be required as to any decision which would have a material financial effect on his leasehold interest where that effect is distinguishable from the decision's effect upon the public generally. If Mr. Hashem becomes a member of the City Council of Calexico and a specific decision presents itself raising this issue, do not hesitate to again seek our advice.

1. 
On August 16, 1982, the candidate entered into a 
land lease agreement with the City of Calexico for a period 
of ten (10) years. 
The lease will terminate on August 16, 
1992. 
He is currently paying a monthly rental of $650. 
2. 
The candidate has announced his intent to run for a 
council seat in the April 1984 Municipal Elections. 
If 
elected in the April 1984 Municipal Elections, the 
candidate's term would be from April 1984 to April 1988. 
3. 
If the candidate is successful in the April 1984 
Municipal Elections, a conflict of interest would be 
created, pursuant to {Calexico's] Conflict of Interest Code, 
requiring disclosure and disqualification of any action 
pertaining to the candidate's lease.
sections.analysis:
The political Reform Act provides that: 
No public official at any level of state or local 
government shall make, participate in making or in any 
way attempt to use his official position to influence a 
governmental decision in which he knows or has reason 
to know he has a financial interest~ 
Section 87100. 
A financial interest in a decision exists whenever there 
will be a reasonably foreseeable material financial effect, 
distinguishable from the effect on the public generally, on: 
(b) 
Any real property in which the public 
official has a direct or indirect interest worth more 
than one thousand dollars ($1,000). 
An "interest in real property" is defined in Section 82033 
to include "any leasehold ••• interest ••• in real property 
located in the jurisdiction • ••• " 
Clearly, in this situation the 
leasehold has a value in excess of $1,000. 
However, because the 
leasehold has less than 10 years remaining, it will not be 
reportable under the provisions of Section 82033 should the 
candidate be elected and take office in 1984. 
However, the 
leasehold interest could form the basis for possible 
disqualification.~1 
II The Fair Political Practices Commission administers, 
implements, enforces and interprets the Political Reform Act of 
1974 (Government Code Sections 81000-91014). 
Government Code 
Sections 83111-83116. 
All statutory references are to the 
Government Code unless otherwise specified. 
2/ 3ee the Commission's Overstreet Opinion, 6 FPPC 
Opinions 12, No. 80-010, March 2, 1981, copy enclosed. 


Eduardo A. Rivera 
December 7, 1983  
Disqualification of Mr. Pat Hashem would be required as to 
any decision which would have a material financial effect1/ on 
his leasehold interest where that effect is distinguishable from 
the decision's effect upon the public generally.i/ If 
Mr. Hashem becomes a member of the City Council of Calexico and 
a specific decision presents itself raising this issue, do not 
hesitate to again seek our advice.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether a candidate for city council with an existing land lease from the city would face additional liability under the Political Reform Act beyond disclosure and disqualification requirements if elected.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC concluded that while the leasehold interest would not be reportable under Section 82033 due to having less than 10 years remaining, it could form the basis for disqualification on any council decisions materially affecting the lease. The FPPC declined to address Government Code Section 1090 questions, directing the city attorney to consult the Attorney General.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: modern; LLM: Document includes incoming request dated November 3, 1983, acknowledgment letter dated November 10, 1983, supplemental inquiry dated November 9, 1983, and FPPC response dated December 7, 1983. The lease agreement is included as supporting documentation. Minor date discrepancy in incoming request (April 1983 vs April 1984 elections) was corrected in FPPC response.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: You have requested the Commission's advice on the following 
question: 
Apart from disclosure and disqualification from any 
action, would any other liability be created under the 
applicable Government Code Conflict of Interest sections? 


Eduardo A. Rivera 
December 7, 1983  
In addition, in your first and then second letters you have 
asked our advice regarding the possible implications under 
Government Code Section 1090, et~. Because we do not 
administer or enforce that provislon of the law,ll we are 
unable to advise you as to those questions. 
You may wish to 
consult with the Attorney General on that point.

CONCLUSION: Disqualification of Mr. Pat Hashem would be required as to any decision which would have a material financial effect on his leasehold interest where that effect is distinguishable from the decision's effect upon the public generally. If Mr. Hashem becomes a member of the City Council of Calexico and a specific decision presents itself raising this issue, do not hesitate to again seek our advice.
embedding.first_500_words: (916) 322·5662 Eduardo A. Rivera City Attorney P.O. Box 1587 Calexico, CA 92231 Dear Mr; Rivera: Administration 322-5660 Executive/legal 322·5901 Enforcement 322·6441 December 7, 1983 Stotements of Economic Interest 322-6444 Re: Your Request for Advice, Our Advice No. A-83-253 Mr. Pat Hashem, a candidate for the City Council of the City of Calexico, has requested that you seek our advice as to a possible conflict of interest situation which might arise if the candidate is successful in being elected to the Council in April 1984. The facts are as follows: FACTS 1. On August 16, 1982, the candidate entered into a land lease agreement with the City of Calexico for a period of ten (10) years. The lease will terminate on August 16, 1992. He is currently paying a monthly rental of $650. 2. The candidate has announced his intent to run for a council seat in the April 1984 Municipal Elections. If elected in the April 1984 Municipal Elections, the candidate's term would be from April 1984 to April 1988. 3. If the candidate is successful in the April 1984 Municipal Elections, a conflict of interest would be created, pursuant to {Calexico's] Conflict of Interest Code, requiring disclosure and disqualification of any action pertaining to the candidate's lease. QUESTION You have requested the Commission's advice on the following question: Apart from disclosure and disqualification from any action, would any other liability be created under the applicable Government Code Conflict of Interest sections? Eduardo A. Rivera December 7, 1983 Page 2 In addition, in your first and then second letters you have asked our advice regarding the possible implications under Government Code Section 1090, et~. Because we do not administer or enforce that provislon of the law,ll we are unable to advise you as to those questions. You may wish to consult with the Attorney General on that point. ANALYSIS The political Reform Act provides that: No public official at any level of state or local government shall make, participate in making or in any way attempt to use his official position to influence a governmental decision in which he knows or has reason to know he has a financial interest~ Section 87100. A financial interest in a decision exists whenever there will be a reasonably foreseeable material financial effect, distinguishable from the effect on the public generally, on: (b) Any real property in which the public official has a direct or indirect interest worth more than one thousand dollars ($1,000). An "interest in real property" is defined in Section 82033 to include "any leasehold ••• interest ••• in real property located in the jurisdiction • ••• " Clearly, in this situation the leasehold has a value in excess of $1,000. However, because the leasehold has less than 10 years remaining, it will not be reportable under the provisions of Section 82033 should the candidate be elected and take office in 1984. However, the leasehold interest could form the basis for possible disqualification.~1 II The Fair Political Practices Commission administers,
embedding.summary: FPPC advice letter regarding conflict of interest concerns for a Calexico city council candidate who holds a 10-year land lease from the city. The Commission advised that disqualification would be required for decisions affecting the leasehold but declined to address broader Government Code Section 1090 implications.
